original_reasoning: Questioning and engaging a matter preserve a clear speech-discussion chain toward dialect or mode of speaking.
Arabic 'to subside, sink, settle' and Greek imperative 'have/hold' meet in the concept of keeping something fixed or steady.

الصيص: كل ما امتنع به (everything one defends/fortifies oneself with - horns, fortifications). ἅλωσις=capture, conquest. Defense fortifications and siege capture are two sides of the same military concept. What you fortify against (صيص) is what ἅλωσις overcomes. Strong military-domain pairing.

بئيس=brave/formidable, بنات بئس=calamities. εὐλαβής=cautious, God-fearing, holding fast. Both orbit the concept of فearing/being formidable: one who inspires بأس (dread) vs one who holds εὐλάβεια (cautious reverence). The emotional core of awe/dread connects them well.
Short, fat, big-bellied strongly matches the result of being well fed.
The Arabic gloss is a plant term, and the Greek item also appears botanical, giving a clear plant-to-plant semantic chain.
Arabic 'sharp, pointed' maps well to Greek 'you have made clear': both share a core of making something precise and distinct.
Arabic becoming fat and filled suggests taking in or receiving, a solid action-result chain.
